'''Andy Haigh''' (born 3 September 1975) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s. He played at club level in the [[Rugby Football League Championship First Division|Championship First Division]] and the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce|sr}} or {{rlp|lk}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Profile at saints.org.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.saints.org.uk/saints/player.php?num=15487|title=Profile at saints.org.uk|publisher=saints.org.uk|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=1 January 2019}}</ref>

==Background==
Haigh was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], England. He retired after a knee injury cut a promising career short. He remained in rugby league as a strength and conditioning coach working with Wigan RLFC, Widnes RLFC and Salford RLFC.  He now works in the medical devices industry.

==Playing career==
Andy Haigh played right -{{rlp|ce}}, i [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 32-22 victory over the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[1997 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 3 May 1997, in front of a crowd of 78,022.<ref name="indy">{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-bradford-have-the-hunger-1259493.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220507/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-bradford-have-the-hunger-1259493.html |archive-date=7 May 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|title=Rugby League: Bradford have the hunger|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|date=3 May 1997|work= [[The Independent]]|accessdate=28 February 2010}}{{cbignore}}</ref>
After retiring from Rugby Andy took up a career in strength and conditioning, where he worked for Wigan RLFC, Widnes RLFC and Salford RLFC. Andy left professional  sport in 2011 to start a new career in  medical devices.
